title: London Lickpenny
summary: CME00103 is a repository XML representation of the University of Michigan encoding of the textual portion of Eleanor Prescott Hammond's 1898 article 'London Lickpenny.' The encoding serializes Hammond's parallel transcriptions of two complete recensions: British Library, Harley MS 542, fols. 102r-104r, on the article's even pages 410-418, and Harley MS 367, fols. 127r-v then 126r-v, on odd pages 411-419. It retains three editorial headnote paragraphs and eighteen line notes, but omits Hammond's introduction on pages 404-409 and her after-text list of later Harley 367 corrections on page 420. The two late manuscript copies are distinct witnesses to one anonymous early-fifteenth-century poem; neither is safely modeled as a physical copy of the other.
review_notes: Hammond's article occupies printed pages 404-420. DIMEV v1.0 gives 404-421, but visual inspection of the complete volume shows that page 420 ends the article and a new article begins on page 421.

The current British Library Harley 542 component description supplies the Harley 367 incipit for this work. Folio range, title, Hammond's edition, and DIMEV securely distinguish the two recensions despite that catalog-level mismatch.
METS describes the Harley 367 copy as executed by John Stow, whereas Hammond describes an unidentified hand contemporary with or slightly later than Stow; the copyist remains unresolved here.

entities.label: General Corpus CME00103 XML
entities.description: Canonical repository XML for the corpus item. It contains two DIV2 recension streams, 32 verse groups, 240 verse lines, three editorial prose paragraphs, and eighteen editorial notes.
notes: The XML revision header records a 2016-09-13 revision, but that date is not evidence for the date of the U-M encoding or of the medieval work.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME00103 digital text
entities.description: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00103. The interface's Author field names Hammond at edition level; she is the modern editor, not the poem's author.

identifiers.scheme: University of Michigan Digital Collections item ID

entities.label: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, London Lickpenny (1898)
entities.description: The immediate print source: an article with introduction and argument on pages 404-409, parallel manuscript texts and notes on pages 410-419, and a list of later corrections in Harley 367 on page 420.

bibliographic.citation: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, 'London Lickpenny,' Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 20, neue Folge 8 (1898): 404-420.
bibliographic.title: London Lickpenny

notes: Hammond rejects the traditional attribution to John Lydgate: she finds no internal Lydgatian evidence and traces the external attribution to Stow and the unidentified Harley 367 heading.

Hammond considers Harley 367 possibly derived from the Harley 542 recension or from its prototype through omissions and transpositions, but states that the exact relation cannot be confidently determined.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 542, fols. 102r-104r
entities.description: The earlier and less redacted of the two surviving late copies represented in CME00103: sixteen eight-line stanzas, 128 lines, in the hand of John Stow.

date_statements.label: Witness copy date
date_statements.value: Fourth quarter of the sixteenth century; the codex is cataloged 1575-1599

holding.notes: The London Lickpenny component is fols. 102r-104r.

physical_description.extent: 168 folios, plus flyleaves, for the whole codex
notes: British Museum binding postdating 1753.
The component occupies only fols. 102r-104r; whole-codex physical data must not be mistaken for work-level extent.
The current British Library component record gives the incipit associated with Harley 367. The folio range and title agree with Hammond and DIMEV's identification of the 128-line Harley 542 copy.
Provenance recorded by the British Library runs from John Stow through the Starkey/D'Ewes collections and the 1705 sale to Robert Harley, then the Harley collection's 1753 national acquisition.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 367, fols. 127r-v and 126r-v
entities.description: A sixteen-stanza, 112-line rhyme-royal recension copied in the physical order fols. 126r-127v but read in the textual order 127r-v followed by 126r-v. Its long heading attributes the poem to John Lydgate and says it was newly overseen and amended.

date_statements.label: Composite volume date
date_statements.value: Mid-sixteenth to first quarter of the seventeenth century; catalog range 1500-1624

date_statements.label: London Lickpenny copy date
date_statements.notes: The normalized upper bound accommodates METS's c.1600-25 wording; the British Library's whole-volume catalog ends at 1624.

holding.notes: The work is physically on fols. 126r-127v, with the recension's textual order beginning at fol. 127r.

physical_description.extent: 187 folios, plus flyleaves, for the whole composite volume
physical_description.format: Composite miscellany in several hands; humanistic cursive predominates
notes: British Museum half-leather binding.
Hammond distinguishes the poem's loose scrawling copyist, a later corrector writing after binding, and one modern pencil correction.

Hammond treats the poem's copyist as unidentified and contemporary with or a little later than Stow. METS's attribution of this copy to Stow is therefore not accepted without further palaeographical evidence.
Provenance recorded by the British Library associates the compilation with Stow, Ralph Starkey, and Sir Simonds D'Ewes before the 1705 sale to Robert Harley and national acquisition in 1753.

entities.label: Walter W. Skeat, Specimens of English Literature, fifth edition (1890)
entities.description: A prior printing of the Harley 367 version. Hammond's retained line notes explicitly compare Skeat's page 25 at Harley 367 lines 39 and 56; it was a selected comparison source, not the sole exemplar for CME00103.

bibliographic.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., Specimens of English Literature from the 'Ploughmans Crede' to the 'Shepheardes Calender,' A.D. 1394-A.D. 1579, 5th ed. (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1890).

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Anglia volume 20 (1898)
entities.description: Complete 552-page scan of the Robarts Library copy of Anglia volume 20. Printed article pages 404-420 correspond to PDF pages 418-434; CME00103's encoded source portion is printed pages 410-419, PDF pages 424-433.

bibliographic.citation: Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie, Band 20, neue Folge 8 (Halle an der Saale: Max Niemeyer, 1898), Robarts Library copy digitized by the Internet Archive.
bibliographic.title: Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie

entities.reproduction_format: PDF scan with OCR text and Internet Archive metadata

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0, record 5987
entities.description: Digital Index of Middle English Verse record 5987 (IMEV/NIMEV 3759), describing both Harley manuscript witnesses. The cached ZIP is the complete version 1.0 dataset; record 5987 is the work portion used here.

bibliographic.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, record 5987, Zenodo record 14585961 (2025).
bibliographic.title: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

notes: DIMEV's bibliographic extent for Hammond is 404-421; the scanned volume establishes the corrected extent 404-420.

entities.label: James M. Dean, London Lickpenny (METS)
entities.description: A later scholarly edition and introduction in Medieval English Political Writings. It is useful for work-level date, form, genre, and recension comparison but is not upstream of CME00103.

bibliographic.citation: James M. Dean, ed., 'London Lickpenny,' in Medieval English Political Writings (1996), Middle English Texts Series digital edition.
bibliographic.title: London Lickpenny

agents.name: John Stow
notes: Securely the Harley 542 scribe and a historical attributor of the poem to Lydgate; not accepted here as the Harley 367 poem copyist.

locators: Printed pages 404-420; encoded source portion printed pages 410-419

access.access_method: Request the physical item for onsite consultation in London through the British Library collection-item form; confirm current reader-pass and availability conditions first.
Request the physical item for onsite consultation in London. The current catalog record states that a letter of introduction is required.

notes: The legacy viewer route is exposed in the catalog, but the catalog states that digital images are currently unavailable.

No public complete facsimile of the work leaves was found in the exact British Library catalog record, DIMEV, METS, or exact-shelfmark web searches. This is a dated search result, not proof that no images exist.

access.access_method: Order digitisation from the British Library. For complete-facsimile research, request all covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and leaves, then record fols. 102r-104r as the work portion.
access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk for reproduction permission

access.access_method: Order digitisation from the British Library. For complete-facsimile research, request the full composite volume and then record fols. 127r-v and 126r-v as the work portion.
access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk for reproduction permission

editorial_practices.representation: Only the parallel recension texts and associated headnotes and line notes on pages 410-419 are encoded.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding omits Hammond's introductory essay and textual argument on pages 404-409 and her separate page-420 list of later corrections in Harley 367.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two manuscript recensions and selected comparison readings
editorial_practices.representation: Harley 542 is printed on even pages and Harley 367 on facing odd pages; footnotes record scribal changes, variant readings, and selected Skeat comparisons.
editorial_practices.description: Hammond presents the two recensions in parallel without collapsing them into a single eclectic text. The XML preserves that separation as two successive DIV2 streams because linear XML cannot retain the printed facing-page layout.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pages 410-420; XML retains pages 410-419 only

evidence.locator: IDG, TEMPHEAD, two DIV2 elements, page and folio milestones, line groups, lines, prose paragraphs, and notes
evidence.summary: Canonical XML identifies CME00103 and structurally contains one 128-line Harley 542 stream and one 112-line Harley 367 stream. Its page milestones run 410, 412, 414, 416, 418, then 411, 413, 415, 417, 419, reflecting serialization of the source edition's facing-page texts.
The CME source repository distributes its contents under the Unlicense public-domain dedication.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00103, 'London lickpenny / [ed. Eleanor P. Hammond].'
evidence.summary: The current item page supplies the CME identifier, title, Hammond edition statement, Halle/Max Niemeyer/1898 publication data, two recension headings, and Public Domain / No Copyright-United States rights labeling.
notes: The interface label 'Author' is edition-level cataloging and is not evidence that Hammond authored the medieval poem.

evidence.citation: Eleanor Prescott Hammond, 'London Lickpenny,' Anglia 20, neue Folge 8 (1898): 404-420, Internet Archive scan of the Robarts Library copy.
evidence.summary: The article supplies Hammond's bibliographic identity, parallel transcriptions of Harley 542 and 367, folio and printed-page mappings, editorial method, prior-edition history, rejection of the Lydgate attribution, and cautious discussion of the recensions' relation.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Hammond, 'London Lickpenny,' printed page 420, in the Internet Archive Anglia 20 PDF.
evidence.locator: PDF page 434, printed page 420; next article begins PDF page 435, printed page 421
evidence.summary: Printed page 420 contains Hammond's after-text list of corrections made by a later hand in Harley 367 and her University of Chicago signoff. It closes the article, is absent from CME00103, and establishes the article's correct extent as 404-420.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API for angliazeitschrif20tbuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete Anglia volume 20 scan, Robarts Library provenance, 1898 publication, file inventory, upload metadata, and possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 542, record 040-002046371.
evidence.summary: Current official record identifies the London Lickpenny component at fols. 102r-104r; dates, describes, and gives provenance for the codex; attributes the manuscript hand to John Stow; exposes a legacy viewer while stating that digital images are currently unavailable; and links current physical-request routes.
notes: Its component incipit appears to be copied from the Harley 367 version and is treated as a catalog error, not a witness identity change.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 367, record 040-002046195.
evidence.summary: Current official record identifies the London Lickpenny component at fols. 126r-127v, quotes its Lydgate heading, dates and describes the composite volume, records provenance, notes that a letter of introduction is required for access, and provides no digitised-content route.

evidence.citation: Dated facsimile search across the exact British Library Harley 542 and 367 catalog records, their exposed digitized-content fields, DIMEV record 5987, METS, and exact-shelfmark web searches.
evidence.summary: No public complete manuscript facsimile containing the London Lickpenny leaves was found as of 2026-07-13. Harley 542's catalog says its legacy digital images are unavailable, and Harley 367 exposes no digitized-content link. An unrelated 1909 facsimile of Harley 367 fols. 110-119 does not overlap this work.
notes: This records the searched routes and date; it does not prove that no institutional or unpublished images exist.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Current official service route for ordering new images; its terms govern supplied images and require permission for reproduction.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, record 5987, 'London Lickpenny.'
evidence.summary: Identifies DIMEV 5987 / IMEV and NIMEV 3759, the discovery incipit, sixteen-stanza description, subjects, and both Harley witnesses with their folio order and stanza forms.
notes: The record's 404-421 citation for Hammond is corrected to 404-420 using the scanned volume.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, Zenodo record 14585961, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14585961.
evidence.summary: Versioned dataset evidence for record 5987, both witnesses, publication/version data, and CC BY 4.0 licensing. The cached ZIP passes archive integrity checks; its 74d7c48 tag-object root corresponds to the annotated v1.0 tag, which resolves to source commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c.

evidence.citation: James M. Dean, ed., 'London Lickpenny,' introduction and text, Middle English Texts Series, in Medieval English Political Writings (1996).
evidence.summary: Current scholarly evidence for anonymous authorship, early-fifteenth-century composition, Estates Satire classification, verse form, and the two complete recensions: Harley 542 with 128 lines in eight-line stanzas and Harley 367 with 112 rhyme-royal lines.
notes: METS's statement that Harley 367 was executed by Stow conflicts with Hammond's direct description and is retained as an open attribution problem.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation.
evidence.summary: States individual-use and reprint-contact conditions for digital editions and licenses all XML under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

open_questions.question: Does the Harley 367 recension descend from the Harley 542 recension, from its prototype, or from another branch of the tradition?
next_steps: Collate both manuscripts directly, including the page-420 later corrections omitted from CME00103.
Review subsequent stemmatic scholarship for evidence beyond Hammond's cautious 1898 proposal.
notes: No copied_from or version_of relation is asserted between the physical witnesses.

open_questions.question: Who copied the London Lickpenny text in Harley MS 367, and how narrowly can that hand be dated?
next_steps: Obtain full-resolution images or inspect fols. 126r-127v in person.
Compare the poem hand with securely identified Stow and Starkey hands and reconcile Hammond's description with the METS claim.

open_questions.question: Can a complete public or institution-supplied facsimile be obtained for either Harley manuscript?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether unpublished or replacement images exist for Harley 542 and Harley 367.
If ordering new photography, request each complete physical codex and map the London Lickpenny leaves with work_portion locators.
Record the delivered terms and fixity before adding any manuscript-image cache.
